resources.string: Start a new session, browse your campaigns, or open the rules reference to begin shaping the story.
Ready to resume from the latest tracker state.
No actions or tracked resources yet.
No spell slots available for this character.
No inventory items available for quick selection.
Tap an action, spell slot, or item to inspect it here.
Set who this character is before you fine-tune the sheet.
Define level, survivability, ability scores, and key combat math.
Review class-driven recommendations, then shape equipment, resources, and actions.
Capture current situation, lasting conditions, and anything worth remembering.
Check the completed builder summary, confirm validation, and save.
Run Loreweaver like a session operator: check encounter state, jump to the next control surface, and keep combat moving.
Use this hub to start a fresh setup, resume an active encounter, or jump to the supporting tools that still need DM attention.
No recent combat update is recorded.
Round %1$d is still in progress. Resume the tracker to resolve outstanding combat actions.
Start a new encounter or load a saved one to stage the next session.
Open encounter setup and begin building the roster.
Open a prepared or paused encounter shell without leaving the operator board.
No saved encounters are available yet.
Saved encounters couldn’t be loaded right now.
No recently saved encounter snapshot is available yet.
• Start with New Encounter when you need a fast scratch-built fight.
• Use Load Encounter to resume the latest saved setup or paused tracker.
• Add terrain, visibility, and encounter reminders in setup notes before initiative starts.
Browse local monster references in a DM-focused library without leaving the session operator flow.
Stage enemies here to build a shortlist before you jump back into setup.
Create a one-off enemy draft for this encounter setup. It stays local to the current staging list unless you promote it later.
Open encounter setup with staged enemies
No reward review is available for the latest encounter.
Send currency to shared party funds
Track short-lived SRD conditions and custom effects that matter right now.
Select ongoing conditions that should stay with the character when saved.
Use this for quick reminders like concentration, wounds, tactical state, or scene notes.
No class spell suggestions for this build.
Use Suggested Spells as Starter Spell List
Use Suggested Equipment as Starter Inventory
Review the full summary below before saving. Recommended identity fields can still be left blank, but required validation must pass.
The air here is thick with a preternatural fog that tastes of old iron and forgotten memories. Every snap of a twig sounds like a dying breath.
A mysterious traveler mentioned a crypt where the gold pieces scream if you pick them up.
The blade finds its mark with a sickening crunch, cleaving through plate and bone alike as a spray of crimson paints the floor.
This will remove all saved log entries.
No campaigns found. Create one to begin your journey!
Campaigns couldn’t be loaded right now.
Campaign couldn’t be loaded right now.
No encounters yet. Tap "Add Encounter" to create one.
Create the encounter shell now, then choose saved party members, add enemies, and add notes from the setup screen.
Monster library import has been removed from this build.
Tap Add to include a monster. Tap its count to remove one.
This note will be permanently removed from the campaign.
Current round and HP are preserved
Resume from Home whenever you’re ready
Your encounter is saved and ready when you return
Session history couldn’t be loaded right now.
No recent encounter summary is available.
Encounter summary couldn’t be loaded right now.
No recent encounter summary is available.
No persistent statuses remain on the party.
No combat log entries were recorded.
The party outlasted the opposition and holds the field.
The party fell or could no longer continue the encounter.
The DM ended the encounter manually before a clear winner was decided.
Save the current encounter state, leave the tracker, or return home while keeping the live encounter available.
Build one shared encounter dashboard: select saved party members, add enemies, set notes, then begin initiative.
Review the roster one last time, then start the encounter.
Continue through the setup steps until the roster and initiative order are ready.
Add at least one party member.
Add enemies, hazards, or neutral turn-takers.
Roster, initiative, and notes are ready for launch.
%1$d staged enemies were imported into this setup.
%1$d staged enemies were imported into this setup, including %2$d temporary drafts.
Review the imported roster below, adjust HP or initiative if needed, then continue building the encounter.
Temporary drafts are already in the roster below, so the DM can tweak them before initiative starts.
Name the encounter, capture location or terrain, and write any scene notes before you build the rest of the fight.
Decide whether this is a quick scratch-built scene, a campaign-linked encounter, or a previously saved setup.
Give the encounter a clear name before launch. Use notes below for terrain, visibility, and other scene details.
Broken stairs, narrow approach, low visibility
Use this for the battlefield snapshot the DM needs at a glance.
Use a fast setup flow for one-off fights or ad hoc combat scenes.
Use this when the encounter belongs to a campaign and needs notes, objectives, and continuity.
Resume a saved or prebuilt encounter shell, then review the roster before going live.
Choose saved player characters and stage any party members that should enter combat.
Add manual enemies, drop in presets, or generate a roster from the local library.
Review turn order, adjust initiative values, and confirm who acts first.
Keep the current roster order exactly as staged in setup. Use this when the DM wants full manual control.
Roll fresh initiative for each participant at encounter start, then sort highest to lowest.
Keep the current initiative values, then sort the roster automatically when the encounter starts.
Capture terrain, objectives, hazards, and reminders before the fight begins.
Use the final review to confirm roster pressure, difficulty, and readiness before initiative starts.
Party %1$d • Enemies %2$d • Ready to launch initiative.
Add at least one party member to unlock initiative.
Select which saved Adventurers are joining this encounter.
No saved Adventurers are available yet. Create them from Characters, then return here.
Add opposition, hazards, or neutral creatures that need a turn in the shared tracker.
No enemies added yet. Use Add Enemy to build the opposing side.
Drop in a ready-made wave to stage common encounters quickly, then fine-tune initiative or add custom foes.
Initiative order will appear here once participants are in the encounter.
Shared tracker ready. Highlight the active turn and keep the next decision moving.
%1$s is active now. Pick the action the table committed to.
%1$s is using %2$s. Tap the correct target on the shared roster.
%1$s is resolving %2$s against %3$s. Record damage, healing, or a miss.
%1$s finished %2$s on %3$s. Log the result, then advance initiative.
Compact roster auto-enabled for larger encounters.
No active participant is available. Start the encounter or advance the turn order to continue.
One-device shared control: players call their move, and the DM records the action, target, outcome, HP, and statuses here.
Use this zone for turn pacing, status tracking, and direct HP or note edits on the active participant.
Repeat the same shared-device loop every turn: confirm the actor, record the declared action, choose a target, apply the result, update state, log it, and move on.
1. The active participant is highlighted. Choose the valid action they declared.
2. Choose the target that action affects on the shared battlefield roster.
3. Apply the outcome, update HP or status as needed, and let the log capture the event.
4. The turn is recorded. End it here to advance to the next unit.
Keep the active unit, next turn, and urgent status changes visible before you move into the deeper control rows.
Stable — no urgent HP or status warnings on the active participant.
%1$d persistent effects are still in play.
Apply the result for this action.
%1$s has been resolved against %2$s. End the turn when ready.
The action result is recorded. End the turn when ready.
This shared tracker is DM-managed: once the result is recorded, end the turn here and hand focus to the next unit.
Undo back to the start of the previous turn using the live turn-history stack.
Current HP: %1$d/%2$d. Enter the exact HP value to record.
Use quick buttons for fast damage or healing. Negative values deal damage, while positive values restore HP.
Expanded roster controls for detailed edits, duplication, and cleanup across the whole battlefield.
No other combatants are waiting in the initiative order.
Tap an ally to focus them, or select them when a turn action needs a target.
No other party members are currently in the roster.
Use this panel to keep enemies visible for quick targeting and minor HP adjustments.
No enemies are being tracked right now.
No combatants added yet. Add your party or individual enemies above.
No notes yet. Expand to add setup details.
Use quantity to add a wave at once. Names are auto-numbered for faster turn tracking.
Build a roster near the current party power.
Close the fight cleanly by choosing the outcome that best matches the table state, then continue to the summary screen.
Choose a status to record as a long-term effect so this character\'s ongoing state stays current between encounters.
Rolling feed for the DM-managed shared tracker: damage, healing, conditions, turn changes, round changes, and quick encounter notes.
No favorite traps yet. Star a trap to keep it handy.
No favorite poisons yet. Star a poison to keep it handy.
No favorite diseases yet. Star a disease to keep it handy.
No favorite entries match this search.
Bundled class reference content has been removed from this build pending provenance review.

plurals.name: spellcasting_spell_list_summary_body
plurals.item: Showing %1$d spell from the selected class list.
Showing %1$d spells from the selected class list.

resources.string: The result is still tumbling through the table…
